title: Alcea leiocarpa
intro: Alcea leiocarpa, also known as the white hollyhock, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family (Malvaceae) native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Alcea leiocarpa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Alcea leiocarpa is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, which is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Alcea leiocarpa is classified in the kingdom Plantae, with the family Malvaceae and the order Malvales. It belongs to the genus Alcea, which is a group of perennial herbaceous plants commonly known as hollyhocks. Within the genus Alcea, Alcea leiocarpa is a species that is native to certain regions. The classification of Alcea leiocarpa at the species level is based on its unique characteristics and differences from other hollyhock species. Further classification at the subspecies or varietal level may be necessary for a more precise understanding of this plant.
